summary: freertc postinstall rewrites consumer package.json to pin itself to 'latest' and re-runs npm install
details: The postinstall script (scripts/postinstall-message.mjs) reads the consumer's package.json via INIT_CWD, scans dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies for a `freertc` entry, and if the version is not already 'latest', overwrites it to 'latest', writes the file back to disk, and spawns `npm install` in the host project. The script also queries registry.npmjs.org for the latest published version. This silently subverts the installer's version pinning and lockfile discipline: any future install of the consumer project will pull whatever the latest freertc version is at that moment, including any future malicious release. The behavior fires at install time without consent and persists by mutating the consumer's source-controlled manifest. This is install-time tampering with the installer's dependency manifest — a self-propagating supply-chain mechanism that degrades the installer's defenses against future versions of this same package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
